abstract: Pass/fail review gate for iOS SwiftUI design and UX quality: two blind reviewers judge screens, diffs, or features against 56 decidable rules derived from the Human Interface Guidelines and WWDC design sessions, working backwards from mandatory rendered evidence — simulator screenshots (light, dark, accessibility text size) and interaction recordings tiled into filmstrips — across navigation and information architecture, modality and flow grammar, layout, color and contrast, typography floors, Liquid Glass placement, feedback states, motion, haptics, and craft. Tier-1 motion rules are recording-primary — code alone nominates candidates, never FAILs, and never prescribes an unseen animation — while the spring-curve and Reduce Motion rules stay code-decidable because their fixes never add animation; gratuitous decoration fails, and every fix must be the minimal change with removal preferred over addition. Targets without a SwiftUI UI surface abort; version-gated remedies go N/A, not FAIL.
